response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned staff-level engineer with a strong trajectory at Google and Meta and a genuine interest in developer tooling, but his core stack is TypeScript/Node.js. Java appears only incidentally across his career (robotic arms, orchestration tooling at Altom), Kotlin is absent entirely, and there is no mention of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM-specific depth. His IDE-adjacent work at National Instruments (a C IDE) and MCP tooling at Google signals relevant instincts, but the foundational JVM and enterprise framework expertise this role requires is simply not there.
strengths: Staff-level engineering experience at Google and Meta demonstrates ability to operate in large-scale, complex codebases
Genuine developer-tooling background, including IDE work at National Instruments and MCP/agentic framework work at Google
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, pairing, CI/CD, trunk-based development) with a teaching track record
concerns: Kotlin is not mentioned anywhere in the resume — a hard requirement for this role
Zero evidence of Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate experience; enterprise Java frameworks are entirely absent
Java appears only at the periphery of his career with no demonstrated JVM internals knowledge or enterprise-scale Java development
